prompt: I have a Python training script at ~/ml/train_cifar.py that takes about 3 hours. I want to run it on a Colab GPU in the background so it survives if my laptop goes to sleep. What's the full setup? The script needs torch, torchvision, and wandb. It saves checkpoints to ./checkpoints/ every 10 epochs.

assertions.name: mentions-colab-new-gpu
assertions.description: Tells user to create a GPU session with colab new --gpu

assertions.name: mentions-upload-files
assertions.description: Tells user to upload script files with colab upload

assertions.name: mentions-nohup-pattern
assertions.description: Uses start_new_session or subprocess.Popen pattern for background execution

assertions.name: mentions-unbuffered-output
assertions.description: Mentions PYTHONUNBUFFERED or python -u for log output

assertions.name: mentions-install-deps
assertions.description: Mentions installing torch, torchvision, wandb dependencies

assertions.name: mentions-relative-paths-exec
assertions.description: Mentions using relative paths with colab exec -f

assertions.name: mentions-download-checkpoints
assertions.description: Mentions colab download to retrieve checkpoints

assertions.name: mentions-progress-monitoring
assertions.description: Shows how to check on running training (pgrep, log tail, or check_progress script)
